acceptance_targets: [chapter_12_prose_negative_reversal_tic] Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:225, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:451, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:471, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:545, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:559 no longer use the 'It/That was not X. It/That was Y.' template, and the full chapter contains no more than two such negative-reversal beats total. Fail if three or more remain, or if any of those five passages still resolves its emphasis through the same formula.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:225, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:451, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:471, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:545, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:559 no longer use the 'It/That was not X. It/That was Y.' template, and the full chapter contains no more than two such negative-reversal beats total. Fail if three or more remain, or if any of those five passages still resolves its emphasis through the same formula.
findings.evidence: The same negative-reversal sentence shape recurs across key beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:225, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:451, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:471, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:545, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:559.
findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on the same 'It/That was not X. It/That was Y.' corrective sentence move. After the first few uses it stops feeling chosen and starts sounding like an audible prose tic, especially because it lands on opening image, sabotage setup, bridge pressure, Paloma's exit, Gabriel's refusal, and the property-cage close.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the repeated turns at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:225, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:451, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:471, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:545, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:559 so those beats differentiate themselves through fresh syntax, image, or clause structure rather than the same negation-and-correction pattern. It is fine to keep one or two strategic instances, but the later emotional pivots should stop echoing the opening move.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the novel's uncomfortable overlap of sanitation, state violence, labor dependence, and family love. When an edge case appears, prefer the version that keeps physical consequence, class coercion, and emotional contradiction visible on the page rather than the version that makes characters cleaner, nobler, or easier to forgive.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene turns away at the exact moment bodily, economic, or moral consequence should become legible, revise toward specificity. Omission is only valid when residue, aftermath, and changed behavior keep the pressure fully present.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms should mark emphasis, bureaucracy, class performance, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate dense procedural chapters with quieter aftermath, meals, night holds, confession scenes, and dark-comic institutional theater. No two consecutive chapters should rely on the same rhythm of scene count, dialogue density, or pressure delivery; vary between confrontation, drift, revelation, ritual preparation, humiliation, public spectacle, and stillness under pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, infrastructural, unsentimental, and specific about procedures without becoming jargon-drunk. Abstract thought should rise from physical consequence instead of replacing it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third anchored primarily to Marisol's bodily labor, practical thought, and moral self-management, with any widening kept chapter-level and earned by scene design.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Let long freighted sentences carry labor process, institutional language, or accumulating sensory load; then cut to shorter, grammatically complete sentences at impact points where a body, fact, or decision can no longer be blurred.
